# Measured Selection Fixture Coverage Audit

**Date:** 2026-06-18  
**Bean:** `csl26-6bul`  
**Generated by:** `scripts/audit-measured-fixture-coverage.js`

## Inputs

- Selection fixture: `tests/fixtures/references-expanded.json`
- Held-out fixture: `tests/fixtures/references-heldout.json`
- Curated import manifest: `tests/fixtures/measured-selection-imports.json`
- Coverage manifest: `tests/fixtures/coverage-manifest.json`
- Fixture sufficiency policy: `scripts/report-data/fixture-sufficiency.yaml`
- Measured-selection sample: `node scripts/report-migrate-sqi.js --styles apa,ieee,chicago-notes-bibliography --out /tmp/csl26-6bul-scorecard-expanded.json`

`scripts/report-data/fixture-sufficiency.yaml` is a checked-in policy file, not
a generated artifact. It is validated by `scripts/check-testing-infra.js` and
records the reference types, scenarios, and fixture sets considered sufficient
for each verification family.

`tests/fixtures/measured-selection-imports.json` records the allowlisted source
items imported from the curated Zotero CSL test-item corpus under
`tests/fixtures/test-items-library/`. The derived `TLIB-*` entries stay CSL JSON
at this CSL/Citum boundary so citeproc-js and Citum score the same source item.
Type overrides are used only to materialize Zotero Extra `type:` hints as
explicit top-level CSL item types.

## Summary

- Selection set: 47 items across 27 reference types.
- Held-out set: 30 items across 26 reference types.
- Disjointness: no ID or title overlap detected; titleless held-out items are
  allowed and checked by ID.
- All fixture-family reference type gaps identified by this audit are closed.
- `references-heldout.json` is registered in `coverage-manifest.json`, and both
  selection and held-out fixtures record their `TLIB-*` import source.

## Measured-Selection Sample

The sample uses the `--emit-evidence` `measured_selection` sidecar field rather
than debug log scraping.

| Style | Section | Selected candidate | XML seed wins | Selection passes | Held-out passes | Interpretation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | ---: | ---: | --- |
| `apa` | citation | `inferred` | no | 220/235 | 130/150 | Citation surface remains strong after fixture expansion. |
| `apa` | bibliography | `thesis-local-default` | no | 28/50 | 8/33 | Type-local win remains visible; expanded held-out now covers rare item classes. |
| `ieee` | citation | `inferred` | no | 235/235 | 150/150 | Citation surface is fully passing for the sample. |
| `ieee` | bibliography | `xml` | yes | 37/47 | 17/30 | XML remains competitive/winning for this sampled bibliography. |
| `chicago-notes-bibliography` | citation | `affix-clear-prefix-10` | no | 84/235 | 50/150 | Citation synthesis improves selection but remains low-fidelity overall. |
| `chicago-notes-bibliography` | bibliography | `affix-clear-prefix-9` | no | 36/47 | 14/30 | Bibliography synthesis remains supported by held-out evidence. |

## Deferred Fixture Gaps

No deferred fixture gaps remain for the reference types and fixture-family
requirements tracked by `fixture-sufficiency.yaml`.

## Operational Conclusion

Measured template selection remains coverage-bound, but the immediate
selection/held-out type gaps are closed with curated CSL JSON rather than
invented records. Future measured-selection changes should keep the
allowlist-driven import path and should add new source-backed `TLIB-*` entries
when a new family requirement or behavior class becomes part of fixture
sufficiency policy.
